title: antirrhinum majus subsp. sempervirens
intro: Antirrhinum majus subsp. sempervirens, commonly known as perennial snapdragon, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the genus Antirrhinum and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Antirrhinum majus subsp. sempervirens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
